title: Deny-capable Codex repo transport fails CLOSED via an outermost bee-marker walk, single-sourced guard root, and guard-side deny of marker-forging out-of-repo writes
action: Make the source-repo Codex PreToolUse (write-guard) transport fail CLOSED and stop resolving its root through git, using an OUTERMOST bee-marker walk and a single-sourced guard root (design B, per plan-repo-fallback-split.md; Gate-2 approved 2026-07-12). MATCHER HARDENING IS NOT IN THIS CELL — it moved to codex-parity-6d. Root cause (validation-codex-parity-6c.md): the transport resolved root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` while the node guard resolves via a nearest-ancestor `.bee/onboarding.json` walk (hooks/adapter.mjs:207 findRepoRoot) — two disagreeing root definitions, opening P1-a (bare repo -> empty root -> fail-open ALLOW) and P1-b/spoof (a both-markers root planted under an allowlisted-writable prefix .spikes//docs//plans/ shadows the real root under nearest-ancestor). (1) In hooks/catalog.mjs, replace git resolution for ALL nine repo-target commands with a POSIX ancestor walk that returns the OUTERMOST (highest) directory carrying BOTH `.bee/onboarding.json` AND `hooks/<script>` — proven to defeat the both-markers spoof in .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/marker-walk-outermost.sh (nearest=ALLOW bypass, outermost=DENY). The walk must terminate correctly at filesystem root (`${d%/*}`, handle `/`). Thread `event` into commandFor -> repoCommand; the plugin branch of commandFor must never read event so the plugin-target Codex and Claude projections stay byte-identical (proven byte-safe). (2) The PreToolUse command passes the resolved root to the guard explicitly: `exec node "$r"/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs --source=repo --repo-root="$r"`. In hooks/adapter.mjs + hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, accept `--repo-root=<path>`: when present AND it contains the `.bee/onboarding.json` marker, use it as the authoritative root instead of findRepoRoot(cwd); when absent (the plugin route), fall back to the current walk unchanged so the plugin path stays byte- and behavior-identical. This single-sources the root and removes the two-root disagreement rather than relying on toRelPath's accidental escape-deny (bee-write-guard.mjs:175-194). (2b) GUARD-SIDE CAUSAL CLOSE of the above-root spoof (round-3, per decision ec629bfe + Gate-2 harden-guard choice; spike .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/guard-deny-marker-forge.mjs and aboveroot-plant.sh): the outermost walk alone only moves the spoof upward — an untrusted agent can plant BOTH markers ABOVE the real root because the guard currently escape-ALLOWS out-of-repo Write/Edit (rel=null -> no deny, bee-write-guard.mjs:211-214), and outermost then selects the attacker root and execs the attacker's guard script. Close it at the source: in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, BEFORE the existing in-repo checks and on EVERY write branch (Edit/Write, Bash-extracted targets, apply_patch targets), DENY (exit 2, corrective) any write whose resolved absolute target is a marker-forging path — basename `onboarding.json` directly under a `.bee/` dir, or basename `bee-write-guard.mjs` directly under a `hooks/` dir — that resolves OUTSIDE the resolved real root. CRITICAL (round-3 red-team, spike redteam-symlink-forge.mjs): containment MUST be REALPATH-anchored, not lexical. A directory symlink planted under an allowlisted prefix (e.g. `ln -s ../.. .spikes/evil`, itself ungoverned + allowlisted) makes a write LEXICALLY in-repo while it PHYSICALLY lands above the real root — lexical path.relative misses it and the marker plants succeed. Resolve BOTH the real root and the target's deepest existing ancestor through fs.realpathSync before the path.relative escape comparison (see the red-team's realParent/escapesRoot fix), and apply the same realpath anchoring to toRelPath so any GOVERNED write whose PHYSICAL destination escapes root is treated as an escape. Legit in-repo and /tmp writes stay PASS-THROUGH (spike-proven, fixedDecision column). Add the symlink plant as a REQUIRED regression arm. Document the residual HONESTLY and wider than before: (i) UNGOVERNED write paths on Codex (unified_exec / native writes — spec Open Gaps), and (ii) GOVERNED Bash primitives that extractBashTargets does not model (dd/install/python -c open/rsync — a pre-existing extractBashTargets gap, red-team-confirmed) can still plant a marker with no symlink. Neither is closed by this cell; both are the D2 'hooks are not a complete security boundary' limit, recorded as Open Gaps, NOT claimed closed. Do NOT represent the marker-forge deny as absolute. (3) PreToolUse fails CLOSED: if no outermost bee root resolves, emit one corrective diagnostic on stderr and exit 2. Pin that literal as an exported constant (e.g. REPO_TRANSPORT_DENIED_DIAGNOSTIC). The eight advisory commands (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, SubagentStop x2, PreCompact, Stop x2) run the same outermost walk but on failure emit the pinned visible diagnostic and exit 0 (fail OPEN) — never exit 2 (a block/turn-control verdict on Stop/SubagentStop, violating B2/R4) and never a bare MODULE_NOT_FOUND crash (violating R2 'visible, never silent' and B1). (4) The advisory diagnostic REPO_TRANSPORT_UNAVAILABLE_DIAGNOSTIC currently says 'no git root' which is now false — reword it to name a missing bee root and update 6b's mechanical assertion + the stale catalog comments to match. (5) Regenerate .codex/hooks.json byte-for-byte from renderProjectionText('codex',{target:'repo'}). (6) hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: (a) REWORK route arms 3 and 4 — off-git resolution inverts them: arm 4 (git off PATH) must now assert resolve-and-run proving git-independence (PreToolUse exit 2, advisories exit 0), and arm 3 (non-git cwd, no bee root above) must assert PreToolUse fail-CLOSED exit 2 + corrective while advisories fail-open exit 0; do NOT keep uniform expectTransportFailOpen on arms 3/4. (b) ADD a below-root spoof route arm: a both-markers root planted under `.spikes/` with cwd nested inside it and an ABSOLUTE apply_patch/Edit to the real .bee/state.json -> assert exit 2 (outermost walk + single-sourced root defeats it; the spoof guard never runs). (b2) ADD an above-root plant arm: from a real bee fixture root, attempt a Write/Edit/Bash/apply_patch that CREATES a marker file (`<above>/.bee/onboarding.json` or `<above>/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs`) OUTSIDE the fixture root -> assert the guard DENIES exit 2 (the plant is blocked at the source), and a control legit out-of-repo write to a non-marker path (e.g. an absolute /tmp scratch file) still exits 0. (c) ADD an onboarding-marker arm: a foreign root that HAS hooks/ but NO `.bee/onboarding.json` must be skipped (proves the onboarding.json half is load-bearing, not just hooks/). (d) split the static codex-repo-target-transport catalog row so 8 advisory commands assert visible-fail-open and the 1 PreToolUse asserts corrective + exit 2; add codex-repo-target-transport AND a new codex-repo-target-pretooluse-failclosed row to REQUIRED_CATALOG_ROW_IDS; add the new route-arm ids to routeRequiredRowIds. Note two couplings the split touches (FEAS): `launchesSourceWrappers` is anchored on `--source=repo$` (test_hook_contracts.mjs:681-683) and will fail on the new `--source=repo --repo-root="$r"` PreToolUse command, and `visibleFailOpen` requires exit 0 in EVERY command (:684-688) and will fail on the fail-closed PreToolUse command — loosen the `--source=repo$` anchor and scope the exit-0 requirement to the 8 advisory commands, not just the `$r` var-name regex. When validating --repo-root in the guard (SEC), require BOTH markers present at the passed root (matching the walk), not just `.bee/onboarding.json`. (7) Finalize docs/specs/hook-runtime.md R2 and B6 (a deny-capable checkpoint fails CLOSED when its transport cannot start; 'unknown' is not 'safe' for a deny hook) and remove the 'under revision pending 6c' qualifier from the scribing Open Gap. Preserve the plugin-target Codex projection and the Claude projection byte-for-byte.

truths: the PreToolUse write-guard transport resolves its root by an OUTERMOST `.bee/onboarding.json` + `hooks/<script>` ancestor walk, never by git rev-parse
a bare bee repo (git rev-parse would fail) denies a gated apply_patch/Edit to .bee/state.json with exit 2 instead of allowing it

with a both-markers bee root planted BELOW the real root (under .spikes/) and cwd nested inside it, an absolute write to the REAL .bee/state.json exits 2 and the planted spoofed guard never runs (outermost walk selects the shallower real root)

the guard DENIES (exit 2) any GOVERNED write that would create a marker file (.bee/onboarding.json under .bee/, or hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs under hooks/) whose REALPATH-resolved absolute target is OUTSIDE the real root, including via a directory symlink under an allowlisted prefix; a legit out-of-repo write to a non-marker path still exits 0
root containment is realpath-anchored (fs.realpathSync), not lexical path.relative — the symlink plant regression arm (redteam-symlink-forge.mjs) is DENIED and is a required test row

the residual (ungoverned unified_exec/native writes, and Bash primitives dd/install/python/rsync not modeled by extractBashTargets) is recorded in docs/specs/hook-runtime.md Open Gaps as a bounded D2 limit, not claimed closed
a foreign root carrying hooks/ but no `.bee/onboarding.json` is skipped by the walk, proving the onboarding marker is load-bearing

the PreToolUse command passes --repo-root to the guard and the guard uses that marker-validated root instead of re-walking from cwd; when --repo-root is absent the plugin route falls back to the existing walk byte- and behavior-identically
all eight advisory commands under a root-resolution failure exit 0 with the reworded visible diagnostic and never emit a bare MODULE_NOT_FOUND crash or exit 2
route arms 3 and 4 no longer assert uniform fail-open: arm 4 proves git-independence (PreToolUse exit 2, advisories exit 0) and arm 3 asserts PreToolUse fail-closed exit 2 with the corrective literal
.codex/hooks.json is byte-derived from renderProjectionText('codex',{target:'repo'}) so any hand drift turns the suite red
the plugin-target Codex projection (hooks/hooks.json) and the Claude projection (hooks/claude-hooks.json) remain byte-identical to their pre-cell committed forms
docs/specs/hook-runtime.md R2/B6 state that a deny-capable checkpoint fails closed when its transport cannot start, and the scribing Open Gap no longer carries the 'under revision' qualifier

artifacts.substantive: event threaded into commandFor->repoCommand; repo transport resolves root via an outermost bee-marker walk, passes --repo-root, fails closed for PreToolUse and fails open for advisories; plugin branch provably event-independent

accepts --repo-root=<path>, validates BOTH markers, uses it as the authoritative root; absent -> unchanged fallback walk so the plugin route is byte/behavior-identical; DENIES marker-forging writes resolving outside the real root on every write branch
regenerated byte-for-byte from the repo-target Codex renderer with the outermost marker-walk transport and --repo-root handoff

route arms 3/4 reworked off-git; new spoof arm (.spikes both-markers) and onboarding-marker arm; transport row split 8 advisory + 1 fail-closed; REQUIRED_CATALOG_ROW_IDS and routeRequiredRowIds extended
R2/B6 record deny-capable-fails-closed as settled; the fail-open Open Gap qualifier is removed

key_links: the outermost marker walk defeats the both-markers spoof — proven by .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/marker-walk-outermost.sh (nearest=ALLOW, outermost=DENY)
the guard's root is single-sourced from the transport's --repo-root, ending the two-root disagreement that caused the incident
the plugin route (no --repo-root) falls back to the unchanged findRepoRoot walk, keeping hooks/hooks.json + hooks/claude-hooks.json byte-identical
prohibitions: do not add apply_patch to the matcher — that is codex-parity-6d
do not let any advisory command exit 2 or crash with MODULE_NOT_FOUND
do not change the plugin-target Codex or Claude projection bytes
do not rely on toRelPath's escape-deny as the spoof defense — single-source the root instead
must_haves.red_failure_evidence: Before edits, reproduce in an isolated fixture and record exact commands + exit codes in this cell's verification evidence: (a) bare bee repo -> current PreToolUse command exits 0 on a gated write (P1-a); (b) below-root: a both-markers root planted under .spikes/ with a spoofed hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs=process.exit(0), cwd nested inside it, absolute write to the real .bee/state.json -> current git-based command exits 0 (spoof bypass); (c) above-root: with the current guard, a Write to an out-of-repo absolute marker path exits 0 (ungoverned), and both-markers planted ABOVE the real root make the outermost walk pick the attacker root -> gated write exits 0 (aboveroot-plant.sh). All three must flip to exit 2 after the fix.

verify: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --catalog-only && ! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && git diff --exit-code 7499a71 -- hooks/hooks.json hooks/claude-hooks.json && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.dropped_reason: Slice stopped by human (2026-07-12): the live incident was already fixed by 6a/6b (merged). 6c escalated a dogfood repo-fallback guardrail into an adversarial spoof-hardening arms race (nearest->outermost->marker-forge->realpath, 3 spike-proven holes) against a malicious-in-repo-agent threat model that D2 explicitly scopes out ('without pretending hooks are a complete security boundary'). Spoof vectors recorded as Open Gaps instead.
